policy_statement: Ensure renewable energy provides 100% of Salt Lake City community electricity needs by 2032.
verbatim_text: Salt Lake City is working to ensure that renewable energy provides 100% of community electricity needs by 2032.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with deadline (100% renewable electricity by 2032).
reasoning: The statement includes a clear, auditable outcome (renewable energy supplying 100% of community electricity needs) and an explicit deadline (by 2032). However, the verbatim text uses weak/non-binding phrasing (“is working to ensure”), which suggests an aspirational effort rather than an enforceable mandate. Under the strict rule, it still qualifies as VALID because it meets the gold standard (timeline + quantifiable target), even though binding mechanism is not evidenced.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: Present — “renewable energy provides 100% of community electricity needs.” Timeline: Present — “by 2032.” Binding mechanism: Not evidenced — text says “is working to ensure,” with no ordinance/mandate/regulatory requirement described. Spatial specificity: Present — applies to “Salt Lake City” and “community electricity needs.” Weak language: Present — “is working to ensure” indicates effort/aspiration rather than a requirement. Strong language: Not present — no terms like “require/mandate” paired with enforcement authority; only the numeric goal is stated. STRICT VALID RULE: (1) Auditable commitment satisfied via 100% RE supply goal; (2) Gold standard satisfied (timeline Yes + quantifiable target Yes); (3) Language strength condition satisfied because weak language is present but the rule allows VALID when gold standard is met (no binding mechanism shown, so confidence reduced but remains >=0.8 given clear metric and date).
